title: Polymorphism, Protocols, and Abstract Contracts · Transfer Build
summary: Support interchangeable storage, notification, pricing, and item-policy implementations. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a protocol-driven circulation application with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use behavior-based polymorphism where callers depend on supported operations rather than concrete class identity.
Define abstract base classes with required operations and optional shared behavior when nominal hierarchy is justified.
Define structural Protocol roles that match consumer needs and support static checking without forced inheritance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
